description:
I design more than interfaces — I design clarity, structure, and meaningful user experiences.

I’m Murali Krishna A, a Senior Product Designer focused on solving complex product challenges through research-driven and user-centered design.

My journey into design began during my CEED preparation at IIT Bombay (2019–2020). With a background in Mechanical Engineering, this experience shifted my perspective from solving technical problems to designing solutions centered around human needs and behavior.

I started my career as a UI Designer at Thryakshari Software Solutions, where I built strong foundations in interface design, usability principles, and structured problem-solving while working on live projects.

In November 2021, I joined Qentelli Solutions as a CX Designer, contributing to improving digital customer experiences through research support, wireframing, and high-fidelity design. Collaborating with product managers and developers strengthened my understanding of usability, user behavior, and enterprise product environments.

During a brief transition period, I took time to upskill and work on independent real-world projects, focusing on UX research, competitive analysis, journey mapping, and usability testing. This phase helped me develop a deeper research mindset and stronger end-to-end ownership in product design.

In November 2024, I joined Invest4Edu as a Product Designer, where I simplified complex educational and financial workflows into intuitive digital experiences. I led the end-to-end UX process and contributed to building a scalable design system that improved consistency and collaboration across teams.

Currently, I work at Mivoyage as a Senior Product Designer, leading the design of an AI-powered Social Travel Intelligence Platform. I drive the complete product design lifecycle, translating AI-driven insights and complex travel data into seamless and scalable user experiences.

I collaborate closely with leadership, product teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to align user needs, product vision, and business goals, with a strong focus on structured UX processes, research-backed decisions, and measurable product impact.
